entries.title: cdn.jsdelivr.net: SSL issue at Quantil
entries.description:
Dec 20, 2021 13:59 CET resolved – Unfortunately today jsDelivr unexpectedly lost its ICP license in China. As effect the Quantil CDN disabled our account.

From now on all Chinese traffic will be served by "near China" locations provided by global CDN providers. Dec 20, 2021 10:26 CET identified – All Quantil traffic was temporarily rerouted to Fastly. Dec 20, 2021 08:40 CET investigating – We're currently investigating an issue with one of our providers affecting traffic in mainland China.

entries.title: Vue 2 is Approaching End Of Life
entries.description: With 2024 almost upon us, we would like to take this opportunity to remind the Vue community that Vue 2 will reach End of Life (EOL) on December 31st, 2023. Vue 2.0 was released more than 7 years ago in 2016. It was a major milestone in Vue's journey of becoming a mainstream framework. Many current Vue users started using Vue during the Vue 2 era, and many great things have been built with it. However, active maintenance of two major versions in parallel isn't sustainable for us. entries.title: Announcing VitePress 1.0
entries.description: Today we are happy to announce the (long overdue) 1.0 release of VitePress ! VitePress is a Static Site Generator (SSG) designed for building fast, content-centric websites. In a nutshell, VitePress takes your source content written in Markdown, applies a theme to it, and generates static HTML pages that can be easily deployed anywhere. VitePress is built on top of Vite and Vue , and is the spiritual successor and modern replacement of VuePress .

entries.title: Why pnpm no longer expands environment variables in a repository's .npmrc
entries.description: pnpm used to expand $ placeholders everywhere it found them — including in the .npmrc and pnpm-workspace.yaml files that live inside the repository you just cloned. That turned out to be a way for a malicious repository to steal the secrets in your environment. As of v10.34.2 and v11.5.3, pnpm stops expanding environment variables in repository-controlled registry and credential settings.

entries.title: Turbopack: What's New in Next.js 16.3

entries.title: What’s new in Svelte: July 2026
entries.description: This month brought a real shift in how SvelteKit projects are configured. You can now define your SvelteKit config directly inside vite.config.js and skip svelte.config.js entirely. We also got the first preview of explicit environment variables, which will eventually replace $env/* modules in SvelteKit 3. On top of that, the language tools and the sv CLI both caught up with Svelte's new {const ...} declaration tags, so the whole toolchain is now in sync. Let's dive in! What's new in SvelteKit Y

entries.title: npmx rainbow comet
entries.description: npmx 0.16 is out! This period was especially full of good news. And it’s particularly great that, amid the constant search for vulnerabilities and risks, we’ve started celebrating more and taking joy in what we do - and what our favorite maintainers and authors do.

entries.title: pnpm 11.10
entries.description: pnpm 11.10 adds the _auth setting for CI-friendly registry authentication, new pnpm prefix and pnpm issues commands, and the ability for pnpm self-update to install pnpm v12 (the Rust port). It also improves pnpm up accuracy, speeds up resolution against registries that ignore abbreviated metadata, and hardens global package management, pnpm deploy, and pnpm pack-app.

entries.title: OpenJS Foundation Security Update: Q2 2026
entries.description: Q2 2026 brought a Node.js 26 release, a major security release addressing 18 vulnerabilities with a streamlined 36-to-7-step process, the removal of the security embargo requirement, and a new LLM-assisted report classifier and web viewer for release management.

entries.title: IoT platform — Total.js
entries.description: The final blog in the IoT Platform series demonstrates how to fully utilize the platform by working with drivers, devices, sensors, data ingestion, visualizations, and integrations. It also explores advanced features such as dataviews, zones, maps, reports, alerts, simulations, and panels to showcase the platform’s complete capabilities.

entries.title: How to install OpenPlatform — IoT platform
entries.description: This blog explains how to install and configure OpenPlatform as the final step in setting up the Total.js IoT platform, allowing you to integrate IoT Platform, OpenReports, and Flow into one unified system. With OpenPlatform, you can manage users, permissions, and apps seamlessly, making your IoT data handling more efficient and secure.

entries.title: Total.js Tables is here!

entries.title: How to install Flow — IoT platform
entries.description: This blog shows you how to install and set up the Flow application as part of the Total.js IoT platform, enabling real-time data processing, alerts, and automation through a low-code visual interface. By the end, you’ll have IoT platform, Stream, OpenReports, and Flow all running together with a basic architecture ready for further use.

entries.title: How to install OpenReports — IoT platform
entries.description: This blog continues the IoT platform installation series by guiding you through installing and setting up OpenReports, a flexible reporting tool from Total.js. You’ll learn how to connect it to your database, create views, and generate reports, enabling you to analyze and manage your IoT data more effectively.

entries.title: How to install stream to IoT platform — Total.js
entries.description: This blog continues the IoT platform setup by explaining how to install and run the Stream application, which collects incoming data from sensors. You’ll learn how to create a driver and device, secure the data with a token, and send real-time values to your platform using a simple API request.

This blog demonstrates its setup, attributes, and integration with other components, using examples to show how to build pop-up forms with menus, content, and buttons.

entries.title: j-Importer - Total.js
entries.description: The blog introduces j-Importer, a component from the jComponent library by Total.js, which allows importing HTML templates, scripts, or styles dynamically based on conditions. It explains how to set up and use j-Importer to import a pop-up form triggered by a button.
